title: KDB Compiled Source Response (per-call model output)
description: Exactly one source -> exactly one response object. The LLM emits this shape per compile call. The contract is wiki-native: pages with slugs, types, titles, and prose bodies carrying [[wikilinks]]. Everything mechanical is Python-owned — summary-slug derivation, link extraction from bodies, page status, provenance, and run-level fields (run_id, success, aggregate errors; see compile_result.schema.json). The model commits only to what it authors.

compilation_notes.description: Optional free-text notes about this compile — e.g. a thin source, an unusual structure, or a tradeoff the model wants on record. Pure prose for the operator's eye; Python never acts on these.

slug.description: Lowercase ASCII kebab-case identifier. No paths, no .md suffixes, no directory separators. The 'summary-' prefix is reserved for the per-source summary page: 'summary-' followed by the kebab-cased source filename stem. Exactly one page per response uses that prefix (the summary page); concept and article slugs never do.

pageIntent.description: One page the LLM wants to create or replace (full-body model, D18). Exactly one page per response has page_type 'summary'; its slug follows the reserved 'summary-' convention (see slug).

body.description: Full markdown body, no frontmatter (Python prepends frontmatter during apply). Use Obsidian wikilink syntax `[[slug]]` inline whenever you reference another page in this response or in EXISTING CONTEXT — concepts, articles, and the summary all link to each other via `[[slug]]` in body prose. The graph's edges are derived from these body wikilinks; there is no separate link list to maintain.
